Read in English Edit

Note

Access to this page requires authorization. You can try signing in or changing directories.

Access to this page requires authorization. You can try changing directories.

Windows 10 release information

In this article

On October 14, 2025, Windows 10 reached end of support. The October 2025 monthly security update was the final update available for the last supported versions:

  • Windows 10, version 22H2
  • Windows 10 Enterprise LTSB editions

This end of support applies to the following editions:

  • Home and Pro
  • Enterprise and Education
  • Enterprise 2015 LTSB
  • IoT Enterprise LTSB 2015

Customers who contact Microsoft Support after this date will be directed to upgrade their device (if compatible) to the latest version of Windows 11 to remain supported or replace with a new Windows 11 PC.

Note

Long-Term Servicing Branch (LTSB) and Long-Term Servicing Channel (LTSC) releases are subject to different lifecycle support policies. Check out the Microsoft lifecycle website for more information.

Extended Security Updates (ESU) program for Windows 10

The Windows 10 Extended Security Updates (ESU) program gives customers the option to receive security updates for PCs enrolled in the program. ESU is a paid program that provides individuals and organizations of all sizes with the option to extend the use of Windows 10 devices past the end of support date in a more secure manner.

For Home Users

For individuals or Windows 10 Home customers, more information about Extended Security Updates for Windows 10 is available at Windows 10 Consumer Extended Security Updates (ESU) program.

For Organizations

Learn more about your options to migrate to Windows 11 or enroll in the Extended Security Update program here.

For more information, see the Windows Lifecycle FAQ.

Windows 10 current versions by servicing option

(All dates are listed in ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DD)

Servicing channels

VersionServicing optionAvailability dateEnd of updates: Home, Pro, Pro Education, and Pro for WorkstationsEnd of updates: Enterprise, Education, IoT Enterprise, and Enterprise multi-sessionLatest update for ESULatest revision dateLatest build
22H2 General Availability Channel 2022-10-18 End of updates End of updates 2026-07 B 2026-07-14 19045.7548

Enterprise and IoT Enterprise LTSB/LTSC editions

VersionServicing optionAvailability dateMainstream support end dateExtended support end dateLatest update for ESULatest revision dateLatest build
2021 (21H2) Long-Term Servicing Channel (LTSC) 2021-11-16 2027-01-12 2032-01-13 (IoT Enterprise only) 2026-07 B 2026-07-14 19044.7548
2019 (1809) Long-Term Servicing Channel (LTSC) 2018-11-13 End of updates 2029-01-09 2026-07 B 2026-07-14 17763.9020
2016 (1607) Long-Term Servicing Branch (LTSB) 2016-08-02 End of updates 2026-10-13 2026-07 B 2026-07-14 14393.9339

Note

The Windows 10 Enterprise LTSC 2021 edition (version 21H2) does not have extended support. It will reach end of updates (also known as end of servicing) on 2027-01-12. Only Windows 10 IoT Enterprise LTSC 2021 (version 21H2) will have support until 2032-01-13.

Windows 10 release history

View a history of all monthly security and non-security preview updates released for Windows 10 versions in support or receiving extended support. Note that the Update type column references each monthly update by the year and the month of its release, followed by a letter indicating the week of the month (B is the second week of the month; D is the fourth week of the month). Out-of-band updates are referenced as OOB. This shorthand notation aligns with what you see in Microsoft Intune. For more information, see Type of update releases.

Learn more about the contents of Windows 10 updates at Windows 10 Update History.


Version 22H2 (OS build 19045)

To update devices running Windows 10, version 20H2 or 21H2 to version 22H2, you can speed up the update process using an enablement package.
